markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI from its prior May 26, 2026 target to November 19, 2026 has extended the window for multiple high-profile events to resolve before launch, boosting trader interest in outcomes tied to music releases, tech milestones, and geopolitical shifts. Recent announcements emphasize the need for additional polish on the open-world title, aligning with Rockstar's history of prioritizing quality over rushed timelines. This shift keeps markets active on developments like new albums from major artists and potential AI advancements, while the November date sits well beyond the July 31, 2026 market close. Traders monitor streaming charts, confirmed release schedules, and official statements for momentum that could alter implied probabilities in this entertainment-driven prediction space.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial declared on May 15, 2026, after a hung jury in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has sharply tilted trader sentiment toward “No Prison Time” at 75.4% implied probability. Repeated prosecutorial deadlocks on the same Jessica Mann allegations, following the 2024 overturn of the original 2020 conviction and a mixed 2025 verdict, have raised doubts about securing additional convictions in Manhattan. Weinstein, already serving a consecutive 16-year California sentence for sexual assault, faces mounting health issues at age 74 while weighing potential plea deals that could limit further time served. Upcoming decisions on whether prosecutors pursue a fourth trial or accept concurrent resolutions remain the key near-term catalysts.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights remain locked in a tight race for the 2026 Stanley Cup according to trader consensus, with their implied probabilities separated by just a few points amid deep playoff runs. The Hurricanes secured the top Eastern Conference seed with a dominant regular-season record and hold a 2-1 series lead over the Montreal Canadiens in the Eastern Conference Finals, bolstered by strong home performance and recent overtime victories. Meanwhile, the Golden Knights completed a surprising sweep of the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Finals, advancing to their third Final in franchise history with efficient defensive play and timely scoring. The Canadiens sit well back after advancing past the Buffalo Sabres but now trail in their series, limiting their market positioning despite earlier upsets. This positioning reflects the wisdom of crowds weighing current form, series status, and matchup implications heading into the decisive stages.

The Canadiens sit well back after advancing past the Buffalo Sabres but now trail in their series, limiting their market positioning despite earlier upsets. This positioning reflects the wisdom of crowds weighing current form, series status, and matchup implications heading into the decisive stages.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's position remains secure heading into the 2027 Party Congress, with recent military and party purges widely viewed as tools to enforce loyalty rather than signals of vulnerability. Ongoing rectification efforts, including high-profile removals of senior PLA figures, have reinforced centralized control without evidence of organized opposition or institutional challenges capable of forcing an early exit. No successor has been designated, consistent with patterns that prioritize extended tenure over orderly transition, while Xi continues public diplomatic and policy leadership into 2026. Traders price the low probability of removal before 2027 on the absence of viable internal rivals, the surveillance state's suppression of dissent, and historical precedent for incumbents extending terms under similar conditions.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the early trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.1 percent, reflecting his status as a sitting governor of a major state with an established national fundraising network and executive record. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10.3 percent, drawing support from the progressive wing, while Kamala Harris holds 8.3 percent amid ongoing visibility from her prior national role. The wide-open field features no declared candidates as of mid-2026, leaving room for shifts driven by 2026 midterm results, primary polling trends among key voting blocs, early donor consolidation, and positioning by other governors and senators seeking broader appeal ahead of primaries and caucuses.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the early trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.1 percent, reflecting his status as a sitting governor of a major state with an established national fundraising network and executive record. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10.3 percent, drawing support from the progressive wing, while Kamala Harris holds 8.3 percent amid ongoing visibility from her prior national role. The wide-open field features no declared candidates as of mid-2026, leaving room for shifts driven by 2026 midterm results, primary polling trends among key voting blocs, early donor consolidation, and positioning by other governors and senators seeking broader appeal ahead of primaries and caucuses.
